user_message: I have this support ticket from Iron Creek Mining that needs map the stakeholders. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into account health.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:09:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:14:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:24:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:28:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:33:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:35:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-O trap (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-O failure mode (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-O -- Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
